pass_type: full re-read of all four design artifacts from scratch; no reliance on iter-4 summaries for correctness verdicts

Config+New primary.evidence: Primary pattern is Config-struct-then-factory throughout: Publisher(nc, max_payload), StreamConfig+create_stream, ConsumerConfig+create_consumer, KeyValueConfig+new_kv_store, etc. BatchPublisher uses 5 KW-only optional knobs (≤6 threshold, deviation 6). No functional-options-as-default anywhere.

Directory layout.evidence: Python adapter layout is src/motadata_py_sdk/{codec,events/{utils,core,corenats,jetstream,stores,middleware},otel,config}/. No new top-level dirs without precedent. resourcepool pre-existing and untouched. Layout is internally consistent with the Python adapter's own established conventions.

OTel wiring.evidence: SDK exposes its own motadata_py_sdk.otel package with init_tracer, get_tracer, start_producer/consumer/internal, init_metrics, Registry, init_logger, get_logger. TracingMiddleware accepts tracer obtained via get_tracer(). No raw opentelemetry.* imports bypassing the SDK's otel layer appear in the design surface. OTELMetricsMiddleware accepts a meter from Registry. Consistent with Go SDK rule (motadatagosdk/otel is the only import point).

Pool reuse.evidence: Python SDK; Go core/pool constructs not applicable. BatchPublisher concurrent flush uses asyncio.Semaphore + asyncio.gather — correct asyncio-native approach. No ants or gobreaker re-imports.

Error sentinels.evidence: 36 EventsError sentinels + 5 CodecError sentinels. _NEVER_RETRY is Final[frozenset[type[BaseException]]] = frozenset({ErrCircuitOpen, ErrRateLimitExceeded}) populated at definition time (CONV-12 fix, iter-3). No custom error struct where a sentinel would suffice. ValidationError raised by __post_init__ validators (existing sentinel, correct). ErrOTELUnsupportedProtocol(EventsError) retryable-by-default concern persists as CONV-9 NOTE (carry-over).

Test style.evidence: package-layout.md shows 110 conformance checks spread across per-feature test files, bench files in bench/, leak files in leak/. Python parametrize pattern (pytest.mark.parametrize) is the table-driven equivalent. No evidence of non-parametrized tests in the design surface (stubs do not contain test bodies). Bench files named bench_*.py — consistent with Python convention (not Go's *_benchmark_test.go, but the Python adapter's own naming rule).

Package godoc.evidence: Every __init__.py has a documented re-export list and/or a module-level docstring in the stub. Python has no doc.go convention; __init__.py module docstrings serve the same role. api.py.stub file-level docstring present. Section-level docstrings present on all 9 sections.

Import ordering.evidence: api.py.stub shows stdlib imports (re, typing, dataclasses, enum, asyncio) before deferred runtime imports (nats, pydantic). No mixed stdlib/external groups within a section. CONV-10 (from __future__ import annotations not repeated at each section boundary) remains a NOTE — implementation files must each carry it; the stub is a single-namespace concatenation.

spot_checks.StreamConfig.__post_init__: api.py.stub lines 924-949: name via _STREAM_NAME_REGEX; subjects non-empty + each via _SUBJECT_REGEX; max_msgs/max_bytes/max_age/max_msg_size >= 0; replicas in {1,3,5}; description len <= _MAX_DESCRIPTION_LEN. Raises ValidationError on all violations.
spot_checks.ConsumerConfig.__post_init__: lines 1070-1097: stream + _STREAM_NAME_REGEX; name + _CONSUMER_NAME_REGEX; filter_subjects via _SUBJECT_REGEX; ack_wait >= 0; max_deliver >= -1; max_ack_pending >= 0; description cap.
spot_checks.TenantConsumerConfig.__post_init__: lines 1124-1161: adds tenant_id via _TENANT_ID_REGEX + MaxTenantIDLength — single source of truth with TenantKVStore/TenantObjectStore per TPRD §15.32.
spot_checks.RequesterConfig.__post_init__: lines 1337-1363: reply_stream + _STREAM_NAME_REGEX; reply_subject_prefix + _SUBJECT_REGEX; instance_id + _CONSUMER_NAME_REGEX; max_payload >= 0; request_timeout in (0, 300.0]; inactive_threshold > 0.
spot_checks.KeyValueConfig.__post_init__: lines 1457-1479: bucket + _BUCKET_NAME_REGEX; max_value_size >= -1; history in [1,64]; ttl >= 0; max_bytes >= 0; replicas in {1,3,5}; description cap.
spot_checks.ObjectStoreConfig.__post_init__: lines 1586-1602: bucket + _BUCKET_NAME_REGEX; ttl >= 0; max_bytes >= 0; replicas in {1,3,5}; description cap.
spot_checks.ObjectMeta.__post_init__: lines 1618-1628: name + _OBJECT_NAME_REGEX; description len cap; chunk_size > 0.
spot_checks.regex_constants: 6 Final[re.Pattern[str]] constants (_STREAM_NAME_REGEX, _CONSUMER_NAME_REGEX, _SUBJECT_REGEX, _BUCKET_NAME_REGEX, _OBJECT_NAME_REGEX) + _MAX_DESCRIPTION_LEN: Final[int] = 1024 declared at api.py.stub lines 213-233 in events.utils §2.1.

spot_checks.ErrCircuitOpen: api.py.stub line 364 — class ErrCircuitOpen(ErrPublishFailed): in events.utils §2.4. 23-line rationale block at lines 339-362.
spot_checks.ErrRateLimitExceeded: api.py.stub line 381 — class ErrRateLimitExceeded(ErrPublishFailed): in events.utils §2.4.
spot_checks._NEVER_RETRY: api.py.stub lines 412-415 — _NEVER_RETRY: Final[frozenset[type[BaseException]]] = frozenset({ErrCircuitOpen, ErrRateLimitExceeded}). Immutable. Populated at definition time. No forward-declare-then-mutate.
spot_checks.middleware_import_form: api.py.stub lines 1813-1820 (CB section) and 1920-1930 (rate-limit section) both carry explicit comments showing import form 'from motadata_py_sdk.events.utils import ErrCircuitOpen/ErrRateLimitExceeded' and confirming the prior cross-module rebind is REMOVED.
spot_checks.no_global_mutable_state: frozenset is immutable; Final prevents rebind. CLAUDE.md rule 6 satisfied.

spot_checks.TracerInitConfig: api.py.stub line 2225: @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True). __post_init__ present (line 2247). otel_protocol: Literal[...]. propagators uses field(default_factory=...) at line 2242.
spot_checks.MetricsInitConfig: line 2305: @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True). __post_init__ present. export_interval default 15.0 (SEMVER-3 unified). default_labels: field(default_factory=dict).
spot_checks.LoggerInitConfig: line 2357: @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True). __post_init__ present. level + console_format + otel_protocol are Literal. module_levels: field(default_factory=dict).
spot_checks.pydantic_sub_models: ServiceConfig, TracerConfig, ReconnectConfig, TLSConfig, StreamSubConfig, EventsConfig, LoggerConfig, MetricsConfig, PublishConfig, SubscribeConfig — all public names (no leading underscore). Mutable list/dict defaults use Field(default_factory=...) form (CONV-4). Confirmed at api.py.stub lines 2455-2593.

per_finding_full_verification.new_scan_scope: Full re-read of all 2618 lines of api.py.stub + all 3 supporting docs. No new convention violations found beyond the 6 carry-over NOTEs established in iter-4.

new_findings_this_iter.detail: No new convention violations introduced. The CONV-5 and CONV-12 fixes from iter-3 remain intact and correct. No regressions detected. SlidingWindowLimiter(limit, window) uses 2 required positional args without a Config struct — within the spirit of deviation 6 (both are required, not optional, so the deviation threshold is about optionality, not arg count); design already documents the pattern; not a blocking deviation.

findings.issue: Requester.create(cls, cfg: RequesterConfig) is the only complex type using @classmethod async constructor. All peer types (Stream, Consumer, KVStore, ObjectStore) use module-level factory functions (create_stream, create_consumer, new_kv_store, new_object_store). The inconsistency will be visible to callers (Requester.create(...) vs await create_consumer(...)).
findings.recommendation: Convert to module-level async def create_requester(cfg: RequesterConfig) -> Requester for symmetry with every other factory.
findings.convention_source: python-class-design skill (new_client factory pattern); consistency with api.py.stub §5.3 and §6.1 factories

findings.issue: otel/ submodules are tracer.py, metrics.py, logger.py (no leading underscore), while every other impl module in the SDK uses _<name>.py convention. This implies these are directly importable public surfaces (otel.tracer, otel.metrics, otel.logger) in addition to what otel/__init__.py re-exports.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) rename to _tracer.py etc. and restrict public access to otel/__init__.py re-exports, or (b) explicitly document that direct submodule imports are a supported and stable public surface for callers who need sub-handle granularity.
findings.convention_source: package-layout.md own _<name>.py convention; sibling packages (events/corenats/_publisher.py, events/middleware/_tracing.py etc.)

findings.issue: ErrOTELUnsupportedProtocol(EventsError) is retryable by default (not in _NEVER_RETRY, not in the non-retryable sentinel list). An unsupported OTel protocol string is a config-validation error raised at init time (resolve_protocol(), TracerInitConfig.__post_init__), not a retryable runtime transport failure. Retry middleware would re-attempt initialization on an unrecognized string, which cannot succeed.
findings.recommendation: Either add ErrOTELUnsupportedProtocol to the non-retryable sentinel list in is_retryable(), or change its base to ValueError (keeps it outside EventsError and therefore outside is_retryable's domain).
findings.convention_source: events.utils is_retryable classification rules; consistency with ErrInvalidConfig (NON-retryable) and ErrMissingConfig (NON-retryable)

findings.area: api.py.stub line 27 — from __future__ import annotations coverage
findings.issue: from __future__ import annotations appears once (codec section, line 27). The stub is a single-namespace concatenation, but each section maps to an independent implementation file. Without this import in each file, forward-reference annotations (e.g., 'Subscription' in Subscriber.subscribe, 'JsMsg' in JsPublisher) require string-quoted form at Python 3.10 and will break mypy strict if omitted.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit note in interfaces.md or a comment at each section boundary: 'Every implementation file must begin with from __future__ import annotations (required for forward references and mypy strict compatibility).'
findings.convention_source: python-class-design skill (mandatory PEP 563 import on every SDK module)

findings.issue: TracingMiddleware.__init__(self, *, tracer, use_otel, sampler) uses all-KW-only args with no Config struct, while every other middleware (RetryMiddleware, CircuitBreaker, LoggingMiddleware, MetricsCollector, OTELMetricsMiddleware, TokenBucketLimiter) follows Cls(cfg: XConfig). The KW-only-no-config form also conflicts with deviation 6 which explicitly applies to classes with '≤6 optional construction-time knobs' — all three TracingMiddleware knobs are optional, so deviation 6 covers it, but the pattern is still inconsistent with the sibling middlewares.
findings.recommendation: Define @dataclass class TracingConfig and change constructor to TracingMiddleware(cfg: TracingConfig) for symmetry with all sibling middlewares.
findings.convention_source: python-class-design skill (Config+New primary); api.py.stub sibling middleware pattern

findings.area: api.py.stub lines 14-15 — file header 'Last edit' comment
findings.issue: File header reads 'Last edit: 2026-05-02 D3 fix loop iter 2 (H5-rev-3)'. The iter-3 changes (CONV-5: ~180 lines of __post_init__ bodies), iter-4 changes (CONV-12: §2.4 relocation block, _NEVER_RETRY Final[frozenset] rewrite), and the iter-5 re-pass cycle are not reflected. Cosmetic documentation gap; does not affect correctness.
findings.recommendation: Update to 'Last edit: 2026-05-02 D3 fix loop iter 5 (H5-rev-3): CONV-5 + CONV-12 verified; 6 open NOTEs' before impl handoff.
findings.convention_source: Pipeline Rule 16 (provenance markers on pipeline-authored symbols); sdk-impl-lead reads this header for handoff context
findings.disposition: ACCEPT-AS-IS — cosmetic only; update before handoff

convergence_rationale: Independent full re-pass (all four design artifacts read from scratch) confirms the iter-4 PASS verdict. Both iter-3 WARNs (CONV-5, CONV-12) remain closed and their fixes are intact. No new convention violations introduced in or after the fix loop. All 8 checked convention rules are PASS. The 6 carry-over NOTEs are unchanged from iter-4 — no NOTE has escalated to WARN. REVISE-strict re-pass finds no delta from iter-4.
verdict: PASS — 0 BLOCKERs, 0 WARNs, 6 open NOTEs (all carry-over; none new; none escalated)
